summary: Scrutiny failed because the new docs portability harness only rejects macOS `/Users/...` and Windows `\Users\...` paths, so machine-local absolute links in other common forms can still slip through the audit gate that this feature was meant to install.

issues.description: `parity_adjacent_docs_do_not_contain_machine_local_absolute_links` does not actually enforce the full machine-local absolute-link contract. It only checks for `/Users/` and `\Users\`, so Linux/CI-style absolute paths such as `/home/runner/work/...` or other machine-local absolute paths would still pass even though VAL-AUDIT-003 requires parity-adjacent docs to reject machine-local absolute links generically.
issues.evidence: The portability scan is hard-coded to `(contents.contains("/Users/") || contents.contains("\\Users\\"))` at `crates/openai-agents/tests/docs_contract.rs:25`, while the mission contract says parity-adjacent docs must not contain machine-local absolute links at `validation-contract.md:170-173`.

codeReview.summary: The feature adds the intended generator-sync, override-liveness, omission-rationale, root-export, namespace, and README checks, but the new docs portability gate is too narrow to satisfy the stated contract across common developer and CI environments.

issues.description: The portability assertion only matches `/Users/` and `\Users\`, so it misses other machine-local absolute paths and leaves VAL-AUDIT-003 incompletely enforced.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The repository's shared `docs_audit` command still omits the newly added `docs_contract` suite, even though this feature makes `docs_contract` part of the audit harness for VAL-AUDIT-003 and VAL-AUDIT-004.
sharedStateObservations.rationale: Workers are told to use `.factory/services.yaml`, but `/.factory/services.yaml:7` still runs only `behavior_parity` and `root_export_parity`. Updating that shared command would keep the mission's documented audit entrypoint aligned with the actual harness introduced by this feature.

commands.observation: Reviewed commit scope and confirmed the feature changed README/docs, the parity generator, and three `openai-agents` audit test files.
Reviewed the full harness diff and verified the new behavior-parity, root-export, and docs-contract assertions added by this feature.
Reviewed the accompanying doc updates and confirmed the commit regenerated the parity ledger plus replaced machine-local links in the parity-adjacent docs.

commands.command: python3 - <<'PY' ... print numbered excerpts from docs_contract.rs, validation-contract.md, and .factory/services.yaml ... PY
commands.observation: Captured numbered evidence showing the portability check only scans for `/Users/` and `\Users\`, the validation contract requires machine-local absolute-link rejection generally, and the shared `docs_audit` command still omits `docs_contract`.
Repository state remained clean aside from the expected untracked `.factory/validation/` output area while performing the review.
